policy.rule: Only entries with status=approved may be used as launch claims. Pending and hold entries must not appear as promotional facts.

claims.statement: Core local scans require no PatchFlow account and do not upload source code when --submit is absent.
claims.limitations: OSV network queries occur unless --offline is used. Backend submission is an explicit --submit path.

claims.statement: Release v0.1.6 provides macOS and Linux archives for x86_64 and arm64, plus Windows x86_64 and arm64 archives.
claims.limitations: The CI onboarding gate executes Windows x64; Windows arm64 remains an artifact-build target, not an onboarding acceptance platform.

claims.statement: The automated install and quickstart path completes in under five minutes on the launch platform matrix.
claims.scope: CI matrix and public quickstart fixture
claims.methodology: Time doctor, vulnerable scan, clean scan, explanation, and SARIF generation after the candidate binary exists; enforce elapsed_seconds < 300 and upload per-platform JSON.
claims.limitations: This is automation evidence, not a substitute for five fresh-user sessions.

claims.statement: Historical v1 benchmark metrics published on 2026-06-27.
claims.methodology: Use the commands, suites, environment, and raw result paths documented in the benchmark report.
claims.limitations: The report contains internal inconsistencies in historical-repository count, LOC, and average timing. Do not use its numbers in launch copy until a reproducible rerun and reviewed correction are published.

claims.statement: At least four of five fresh users reach a useful finding in under five minutes.
claims.methodology: Start timing before installation; stop when the participant can identify and explain one useful result. Record failures and anonymize notes.
claims.limitations: No sessions have been claimed or counted by automation.
